payload.title: Generalize canonical inbox as scale-relative governed crossing substrate
payload.summary: Discussion of builder-to-architect handoff initially suggested an outward canonical inbox plus an inward internal inbox. Reading Scale-Relative Operation Topology sharpened this: these are not separate species. They are the same inbox pattern instantiated at different scale positions. Narada should preserve one canonical envelope substrate while enriching envelope structure with scale-relative crossing coordinates and message kinds.
evidence: Originating incident: builder completed work and commits but did not leave an explicit architect review_request. This exposed a missing intra-Site role crossing, not necessarily a need for a separate inbox ontology.

Scale-Relative Operation Topology states that internally an Operation is a topology of authority zones and governed crossings, while externally it may appear as one zone-like participant; the outer reading must not erase the inner topology.

Applied here: the User Site may appear externally as one Site, but internally architect and builder remain distinct loci. Builder-to-architect review is still a governed crossing and should use the same canonical envelope grammar.
proposal: Keep canonical inbox as one governed crossing substrate / envelope grammar. Do not create unrelated inbox species for external intake vs internal handoff. Instead, enrich envelopes with typed scale-relative crossing metadata.

Add or formalize crossing coordinates on canonical envelopes: crossing.scale, crossing.authority_scope, crossing.from_locus, crossing.to_locus, crossing.owning_site, crossing.target_authority, crossing.requested_crossing, and responsibility/review/admission state as appropriate.
Initial crossing scales may include site, role, operation, subsystem, and external. Initial authority scopes may include same_site, cross_site, external_to_site, and intra_operation.
Initial message kinds should include existing observation/proposal plus review_request, handoff, approval_request, admission_request, verification_request, blocker, capa_candidate, and capa_addendum.
Views such as architect inbox, builder handoff queue, Narada proper inbox, and CAPA queue should be projections over the canonical envelope substrate, not separate ontological inboxes.
Guardrail: one envelope representation must not imply one global authority. Each envelope still resolves to the local authority of its target locus, and admission remains governed at that locus.
payload.recommendation: Promote as doctrine/schema work adjacent to canonical inbox, governed crossing, CAPA Operation, Site factorization, and Scale-Relative Operation Topology. Use the builder handoff incident as the first test case.

promotion.note: recorded_pending_crossing: executable promotion for target kind 'decision' is not implemented yet.
